mechanism_narrative: Rising federal funds rates increase the discount rate applied to future corporate earnings, mechanically depressing equity valuations and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Walk-forward Rank IC remains below 0.02 over any 3-year rolling window, or positive 20-day returns exceed negative returns in more than 55% of observations.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month increase in the unemployment rate exceeding 0.2 percentage points signals deteriorating labor market conditions and impending recession, triggering risk-off flows that produce negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Forward returns are positive in more than 60% of triggered instances or the signal loses statistical significance with a p-value greater than 0.10 in out-of-sample testing.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous expansion in retail sales and housing starts indicates synchronized strength across consumer discretionary and fixed investment demand, driving corporate revenue growth expectations and generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The joint signal produces negative returns in more than 50% of cases or the interaction term coefficient is statistically insignificant in multivariate regression.

mechanism_narrative: During periods of monetary easing, rising industrial production reflects improving manufacturing capacity utilization and supply-side momentum, which translates into positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days as markets price in near-term earnings revisions.
failure_condition: The signal reverses direction during easing cycles or fails to achieve a positive Sharpe ratio greater than 0.5 in walk-forward simulations.

mechanism_narrative: When the consumer price index surpasses 198.0, persistent inflationary pressures force central banks to maintain restrictive policy, compressing equity multiples and generating negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: Returns remain positive in more than 55% of threshold-crossing events or the signal decays to zero predictive power within 10 trading days.

mechanism_narrative: Sustained growth in total nonfarm payrolls reflects expanding economic activity and rising household income, which supports corporate consumption and investment, producing positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: The correlation between payroll growth and forward returns turns negative or Rank IC falls below 0.01 over rolling 3-year windows.

mechanism_narrative: Concurrent acceleration in real GDP and retail sales confirms broad-based demand expansion, reducing recession probabilities and driving positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days as equity risk premiums compress.
failure_condition: The combined signal yields negative returns in more than 50% of observations or the interaction effect is statistically indistinguishable from zero.

mechanism_narrative: In late-cycle expansions, a divergence where industrial production declines while unemployment rises signals supply-side contraction and labor market weakness, triggering negative S&P 500 forward returns over the next 5 trading days due to recession repricing.
failure_condition: The signal fails to predict negative returns in more than 60% of late-cycle divergence episodes or loses predictive power when tested out-of-sample.
